svg.title: Structure of IRDAI Actuarial Finance Investment Functions Regulations 2024 and Master Circular
svg.desc: A structural diagram showing the chapters, schedules and sections of the 2024 IRDAI regulation and its companion Master Circular

g.text: IRDAI (Actuarial, Finance and Investment Functions of Insurers) Regulations, 2024
F. No. IRDAI/Reg/10/204/2024 — Notified 20 Mar 2024, effective 1 Apr 2024 — 446 pages

svg.text: Short title, commencement, applicability
Objectives (4 key objectives)
Definitions — UPR, PDR, URR, Technical Liabilities, RSM…
Principles for actuarial, finance and investment functions
Board-approved policies required for each function
References to Schedules I, II, III, IV and V
Power to issue circulars and clarifications
Repeal of 9 older regulations (incl. ALSM 2016, FS Regs 2002)
Schedules (substantive content)
★ Part IV — key for health insurers: URR, UPR, PDR, IBNR, IBNER, RSM
★ Part II Cl.4 — UPR calculation: 50% NWP or proportion of unexpired period
Approved and other investments, exposure norms
ALM, risk management, valuation of securities
Reporting formats (IRDA-GI-SM, GI-TA, GI-TR etc.)
Loans to employees — eligible purposes, limits
Board-approved policy requirement
Procedure for inspection and supply of copies of returns

g.text: Master Circular on Actuarial, Finance and Investment Functions of Insurers

svg.text: ★ Section III — guidance on URR, UPR, PDR for standalone health insurers
Sec I–II: Introduction and accounting/disclosure requirements
Sec III: Life insurer specific accounting
Sec IV: GI, health and reinsurer specific accounting
Sec V–VII: FRBs, Lloyd's, format deviations
Sec I–II: Specific investment categories and risk management
Sec III–IV: Valuation guidelines and operational procedure
Sec V–VI: Disclosure and reporting norms
Sec VII–VIII: Repealed circulars and category codes
★ = sections most relevant to health insurance reserving (UPR, PDR, URR)
